channel.title: Lionpoint Partners — Market Pulse
channel.description: Daily read on the US legal lateral market.

item.title: McGuireWoods pulls a Kirkland debt finance partner in Houston, and firms keep stacking dealmakers
item.description: McGuireWoods recruited a debt finance partner from Kirkland & Ellis in Houston. Sullivan & Cromwell added a national security and trade partner in...

item.title: Latham takes a third Davis Polk capital markets partner, and Paramount's Warner deal is paused
item.description: Latham & Watkins poached another Davis Polk & Wardwell capital markets partner in New York, its third from that practice this month, and the same day A&O...

item.title: A quiet Monday: Polsinelli adds real estate in Dallas, and data breach suits reach Blank Rome
item.description: On the business side, Blank Rome is facing new lawsuits over a data breach, part of a string of cases triggered by recent cyberattacks against law firms,...

item.title: Jones Walker absorbs a Texas corporate boutique, and K&L Gates cuts 10% of its staff
item.description: Jones Walker absorbed a Texas corporate boutique, picking up a three-partner M&A and private equity team in Houston from Walker Eisenbraun and naming one...

item.title: The written cut: Linklaters raids Paul Weiss, and Sidley pulls a Latham finance partner
item.description: As many of you know, I'm originally from Argentina. Here's the run in writing. Sidley Austin poached a structured finance partner from Latham & Watkins in...

item.title: Simpson Thacher opens Dallas with an Akin lift-out, and Goodwin plants a flag in San Diego
item.description: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett opened a new Dallas office by lifting a three-partner hybrid capital team out of Akin Gump, and installed one of the arrivals...

item.title: Latham raids Baker Botts for an energy team in Houston
item.description: Latham & Watkins is poised to hire a group of partners from Baker Botts in Houston, including a veteran energy deals partner. It's the latest in a busy...

item.title: Simpson Thacher raids Clifford Chance's insurance team, and Paul Hastings jumps into sports
item.description: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett hired a team of insurance M&A partners from Clifford Chance and Mayer Brown in New York. The two Clifford Chance partners...

item.title: Latham poaches a Davis Polk capital markets pair, including the SpaceX IPO lead
item.description: Latham & Watkins poached a two-partner capital markets team from Davis Polk & Wardwell, one of its top rivals for that work, landing the partner who led...

item.title: Sidley keeps raiding Clifford Chance, this time a five-partner finance group
item.description: Sidley Austin kept up its raid on Clifford Chance, poaching a five-partner finance group across New York and Washington, centered on structured and...

item.title: Kirkland and Cravath each lose partners, and Latham pulls further ahead on IPOs
item.description: Kirkland & Ellis lost two partners: Dechert LLP took a private equity M&A partner in San Francisco, and Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP took a fund formation...

item.title: Sidley keeps raiding rivals' capital markets benches, and Latham loses two partners in a day
item.description: Sidley Austin kept up its capital markets hiring raid on rivals, adding a top high-yield partner from Latham & Watkins. Latham lost a second partner the...

item.title: Paul Hastings grabs Cahill's private credit co-head, and Katten plants a flag in Miami
item.description: Paul Hastings hired the co-head of Cahill Gordon & Reindel's private credit practice in New York, a lawyer who also spent time as a managing director at...

item.title: Big Law's hottest new job is a director of AI, and firms can't fill it
item.description: On the business side, a Delaware judge ruled that JPMorgan must keep paying Charlie Javice's legal bills, now north of $74 million, after finding the bank...

item.title: Orrick lifts a six-lawyer fintech team out of Greenberg Traurig in Miami
item.description: Orrick pulled a six-lawyer fintech team from Greenberg Traurig in Miami, its first big hire since opening there last year. Latham & Watkins recruited...

item.title: Gordon Rees lifts out eight litigation partners in Northern California
item.description: Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani brought in eight litigation partners across its Northern California offices, one of the bigger lift-outs of the week. Paul,...

item.title: Dechert pulls two special situations partners from Ropes & Gray
item.description: Dechert LLP recruited two special situations partners from Ropes & Gray LLP, building out its distressed finance and liability management work. King &...

item.title: Debevoise pulls a Ropes & Gray finance partner, and Robins Kaplan grabs four ex-DOJ lawyers
item.description: Debevoise is bringing in a Ropes and Gray finance partner in New York to build out its finance bench. Robins Kaplan added four former DOJ attorneys in...

item.title: Paul Weiss lands the partner who built Kirkland's restructuring practice
item.description: Paul Weiss is bringing in the partner credited with building Kirkland's restructuring practice, one of the most recognized bankruptcy names in the...

item.title: Davis Polk pulls Hogan Lovells' M&A chief days before the Cadwalader merger closes
item.description: Davis Polk hired the global head of Hogan Lovells' M&A practice as a partner in Washington, and he is walking out after three decades just days before...

item.title: White & Case keeps buying, and Goodwin loses two partners in one day
item.description: White & Case hired the co-leader of Goodwin's secondaries practice as a partner in New York, part of an aggressive US buildout that has brought on more...

item.title: Paul Hastings hits eight PE partners in eight months, and the deal-side wire stays hot
item.description: Paul Hastings pulled a private equity partner out of Dechert in New York, and by the firm's own count that's the eighth PE partner it has added in eight...

item.title: A finance co-head is reportedly headed to Dechert, and state AGs circle the Paramount deal
item.description: The bigger headline is in the courts, where California's attorneys general are reportedly looking at two former FTC lawyers at Milbank to help challenge...

item.title: Houston turns into a deal-lawyer dogfight, and firms race to staff up on dealmakers
item.description: Simpson Thacher pulled an energy and infrastructure deals partner out of Latham in Houston, and Latham also added a capital markets partner from A&O...

item.title: Sidley pulls a finance partner out of Wachtell, and DC antitrust hiring runs hot
item.description: The poach of the day was Sidley pulling a global finance partner out of Wachtell in New York. Washington was all about antitrust: Sullivan and Cromwell...
